promise_text_en: Kalaignar Magalir Urimai Thittam (Women's Rights Scheme) implemented
impact_mechanism: Legal and institutional framework establishes enforceable rights (property ownership, workplace protection, political participation) that remove structural barriers to women's economic and social participation.
first_order_effect: Defined legal entitlements for ~3.9 crore women in TN; immediate clarity on property rights, workplace grievance redressal, and representation quotas in local bodies and state institutions.
second_order_effect: Increased women's asset ownership and income-generating capacity as property rights are formalized; higher female workforce participation as workplace protections reduce harassment and unsafe working conditions.
third_order_effect: Sustained reduction in gender wealth gap and intergenerational poverty as women accumulate productive assets and pass them to children; structural shift in household bargaining power favoring women's education and health investment.

promise_text_en: Free women's transport (Kalaignar Magalir Urima Travel) - free/discounted bus journeys for women
impact_mechanism: Subsidy/waiver on transit costs reduces the transport cost barrier that restricts women's access to employment, education, and healthcare, enabling mobility.
first_order_effect: ~1.9 crore women (assuming ~50% of daily commuters) gain free/discounted bus access; estimated savings of ₹20–40/day per user (₹5,000–10,000/year), reducing out-of-pocket mobility costs.
second_order_effect: Higher female labor force participation (target: +2–3 percentage points in urban LFPR from current 33%) as commute costs fall; increased school attendance for girls in peripheral areas.
third_order_effect: Modest income growth for beneficiary households; sustained female workforce participation reduces skill depreciation and wage gaps; long-term demographic benefit if mobility gains translate to delayed marriage and fertility decline.

promise_text_en: Chief Minister's Midday Meal Scheme (Primarry, Upper Primary, Secondary and Higher Secondary Schools)
impact_mechanism: Free meal provision removes caloric/nutritional barrier to school enrollment and attendance, enabling children to learn and grow without nutritional stress.
first_order_effect: ~1.2 crore school children (primary through higher secondary) receive daily cooked meals; eliminates user cost of nutrition for poorest 60–70% of student population.
second_order_effect: Reduced school dropout rates (especially among girls and SC/ST students); improved learning outcomes as classroom attention/cognitive capacity increases with nutrition; higher attendance and completion rates.
third_order_effect: Cohort-level earnings gain (~3–5% lifetime earnings lift per year of meal program exposure); reduced intergenerational poverty as educated cohorts enter labor market with higher productivity; lower healthcare costs as malnutrition-linked diseases decline.

promise_text_en: Tamil Pudhuvai (Tamil New Generation) - new education initiative
impact_mechanism: Curriculum reform and pedagogical innovation improve the relevance and quality of Tamil-medium education, raising learning outcomes and completion rates for vernacular students.
first_order_effect: Redesigned curriculum and teaching methods implemented across TN schools; measurable improvement in foundational literacy and numeracy (baseline unknown, but target ~10–15 percentage point gain in learning levels); enhanced cultural/civic content.
second_order_effect: Higher completion rates and board exam pass rates for Tamil-medium cohorts; improved transition to skilled employment as curriculum aligns with labor market needs; reduced skill-to-job mismatch.
third_order_effect: Long-term wage and productivity gains for vernacular cohorts; reduced caste-based wage discrimination as Tamil-medium students gain credentials; demographic shift toward higher human capital among marginalized groups.

promise_text_en: Naan Muthalvan (I am the Chief Minister) - digital literacy and skill development program
impact_mechanism: Digital literacy and vocational skill training provide unemployed/underemployed youth with market-relevant skills, enabling transition to formal employment or self-employment.
first_order_effect: Training delivered to ~5–10 lakh youth (estimated based on typical state scheme reach); participants gain digital competency (basic/intermediate) and job-specific skills in 2–4 month modules.
second_order_effect: ~30–40% of trainees secure formal/informal employment within 6–12 months post-training; income increase of ₹2,000–6,000/month for employed beneficiaries; reduced youth unemployment.
third_order_effect: Wage growth and labor force participation stabilization for young cohorts; reduced migration to other states as local job opportunities improve; intergenerational mobility as skilled youth earn higher incomes and educate children better.

promise_text_en: Makkalai Thedi Maruthuvam (Medicine reaching the people) - door-to-door healthcare and wellness program
impact_mechanism: Door-to-door preventive healthcare and medication access reduces the travel/opportunity cost of seeking healthcare, enabling early detection and chronic disease management in underserved rural/urban poor populations.
first_order_effect: Health workers conduct ~2–4 visits per household per year to ~1.5–2.0 crore low-income households; basic screening, medication, and health education delivered at doorstep; ~20–30% of rural/urban poor households gain access to preventive care they previously could not afford.
second_order_effect: Early detection of non-communicable diseases and reduced disease burden; lower out-of-pocket spending on crisis/curative care; reduced catastrophic health expenditure as preventive care reduces hospitalizations.
third_order_effect: Improved productivity and reduced poverty-driven hospitalization debt; health workforce absorption of ~50,000–100,000 ASHA/health workers creates employment; long-term life expectancy and quality-of-life gains for poor populations.

promise_text_en: Innyuyir Kalappu - scheme to improve livelihood of farmers and agricultural workers (48 components)
impact_mechanism: Multi-component livelihood scheme (48 components) addresses fragmented pain points in agricultural production, marketing, and income stability through integrated support (inputs, tech, credit, insurance, market linkage).
first_order_effect: ~10–15 lakh farmers and agricultural workers enroll; receive bundled support across 48 components (e.g., subsidized seeds, soil testing, drip irrigation, crop insurance, cooperative linkage); estimated income uplift of ₹30,000–60,000/year per beneficiary.
second_order_effect: Reduced input costs and improved yields (estimated 10–15% productivity gain); higher net farm income reduces debt-driven distress sales; improved food security and reduced seasonal income volatility for agricultural workers.
third_order_effect: Stabilization of farm livelihoods and reduced farm distress migration; sustained consumption smoothing enables children's education; modest reductions in farmer suicide rates if debt trap is broken.

promise_text_en: Separate Finance Department for Farmers (Separate Finance Department announced for farmers)
impact_mechanism: Dedicated finance department for agriculture improves policy coordination, subsidy targeting, and credit disbursement efficiency, reducing bureaucratic delays and fragmentation in farm support delivery.
first_order_effect: New administrative structure with dedicated budget allocation (~₹5,000–8,000 cr estimated for ag sector); faster processing of crop loans, subsidy claims, and insurance payouts; reduced inter-agency coordination gaps.
second_order_effect: Faster credit disbursement and subsidy claim resolution (target: 50–70% reduction in processing time); improved timeliness of input support during planting seasons; farmer satisfaction with scheme accessibility rises.
third_order_effect: Sustained farmer participation in government schemes as trust in delivery improves; modest productivity gains as timely inputs reduce missed sowing windows; reduced informal debt as formal credit flows more reliably.

promise_text_en: Kalaignar Kavirai Thittam (Women's Empowerment Scheme)
impact_mechanism: Direct income or skill-building transfer to women, expanding economic agency and reducing dependency on male household members.
first_order_effect: Women in TN (target likely 10-20 lakh based on comparable schemes) receive cash stipends, vocational training, or entrepreneurship support, increasing household income or earning capacity.
second_order_effect: Increased female labor force participation and household bargaining power reduce fertility rates and improve child health outcomes through maternal decision-making authority.
third_order_effect: Over 10+ years, higher female education and workforce participation create a persistent demographic dividend and reduced dependency on social safety nets.

promise_text_en: Anbukkieyirangal Thittam (Affection Relationships Scheme) - marriage assistance and social support
impact_mechanism: Marriage subsidy or financial incentive reduces upfront wedding costs for poor households, eliminating a major debt trigger and reducing bride dowry pressure.
first_order_effect: ~3-5 lakh marriages (estimated from BPL households) receive direct cash assistance (₹50K-1L per couple), avoiding informal debt or dowry transactions.
second_order_effect: Reduced marriage-related indebtedness in poor households increases consumption capacity for food/health; dowry reduction lowers physical/emotional abuse risk for brides.
third_order_effect: Normalization of government-funded marriage breaks dowry-debt cycle across generations, but effect limited if scheme does not simultaneously criminalize/enforce dowry laws.

promise_text_en: Anbu Sathaisal Thittam (Affectionate Feeding Scheme) - nutritious meal distribution
impact_mechanism: In-kind nutrition transfer (meals) at school or community level removes malnutrition as a barrier to attendance and cognitive development.
first_order_effect: ~2-3 crore school children and vulnerable adults receive daily nutritious meals, immediately improving caloric intake and reducing stunting/wasting prevalence.
second_order_effect: Better-nourished children show improved school attendance and learning outcomes; reduced disease burden decreases healthcare costs for poor households.
third_order_effect: Sustained nutrition support over 5+ years raises human capital formation; early childhood investments compound into higher earnings and reduced non-communicable disease burden in adulthood.

promise_text_en: Tholazi Vidudhikal (Language Hostels) - hostel facilities for students learning mother tongues
impact_mechanism: Residential subsidized housing for students removes the cost barrier to mother-tongue education, enabling enrollment in regional language programs.
first_order_effect: ~10K-50K students (estimated from mother-tongue learner pool) access hostels, enabling attendance at Tamil/regional language schools without family relocation.
second_order_effect: Increased enrollment in Tamil-medium schools preserves linguistic identity and expands regional-language workforce; hostel students face lower dropout due to reduced family financial stress.
third_order_effect: Stronger Tamil-language proficiency and cultural continuity in workforce; marginal employment premium for mother-tongue education if linked to skill certification.

promise_text_en: Direct appointment of differently-abled persons in local government bodies
promise_text_ta: உள்ைலாடசி அलमppुकलिल मलाற्ற् तिरनलालिकळक्कु तनेरटि निअमनम्
impact_mechanism: Reservation-backed employment guarantee provides income-stable public sector jobs to persons with disabilities, removing earnings volatility and labor-market discrimination.
first_order_effect: ~1000-5000 differently-abled persons are appointed to local government bodies (panchayats, municipalities) with stable wages, health insurance, and pension eligibility.
second_order_effect: Improved household income stability reduces poverty risk; direct representation in governance creates accountability for disability-inclusive policy and infrastructure design.
third_order_effect: Normalized disabled participation in public service creates cultural shift toward inclusion; local bodies design barrier-free infrastructure, improving mobility and economic access for all disabled persons.

promise_text_en: Ayyathiranidasan Pandithar Kudiyiruppu Scheme - housing and residential welfare program
impact_mechanism: Subsidized or free housing provision removes shelter insecurity and eliminates monthly rent burden, freeing income for food and healthcare.
first_order_effect: ~1-2 lakh vulnerable households (elderly, widows, marginalized groups targeted) receive housing units or housing subsidies, securing tenure and reducing homelessness.
second_order_effect: Reduced housing cost burden improves household food security and health spending; secure housing tenure increases collateral availability and enables micro-credit uptake.
third_order_effect: Over 10+ years, intergenerational asset accumulation (owned housing as inheritance) reduces wealth inequality; stable housing improves child enrollment and learning outcomes.

promise_text_en: Thalaigudi Thittam - housing scheme for economically weaker sections
impact_mechanism: Subsidized or free housing for economically weaker sections eliminates shelter cost as a poverty perpetuator, enabling reallocation of income to education and health.
first_order_effect: ~2-5 lakh EWS households receive constructed housing or land grants, reducing housing cost from ~30-40% of income to near-zero.
second_order_effect: Released household income flows to child education and preventive healthcare; asset ownership (housing) improves social dignity and credit access for microenterprises.
third_order_effect: Generational wealth accumulation through owned housing; improved child enrollment and learning outcomes reduce inter-generational poverty transmission.

promise_text_en: Professor Ambazhan Education Infrastructure Housing Scheme
scheme_name: Professor Ambazhan Education Infrastructure Housing Scheme
impact_mechanism: Housing provision for education sector workers (teachers, staff) stabilizes their income utilization and improves residence proximity to schools, reducing commute burden.
first_order_effect: ~10K-50K education sector employees (teachers, support staff) receive housing units, reducing monthly rent expense and stabilizing residential security.
second_order_effect: Improved teacher housing stability and reduced commute time may increase classroom presence and teaching quality; direct benefit primarily to workforce, not students.
third_order_effect: Marginal improvement in teacher retention and morale if housing is near worksite; limited systemic impact on educational outcomes unless combined with pedagogy/curriculum reform.

promise_text_en: Perundhalaivar Kamaraj College Housing Scheme
impact_mechanism: On-campus housing provision reduces out-of-pocket accommodation costs for college students from economically disadvantaged backgrounds, enabling sustained enrollment and academic focus.
first_order_effect: College students from poor/lower-middle-income households gain subsidized/free hostel access, reducing dropout pressure from housing unaffordability.
second_order_effect: Improved course completion rates and academic performance among beneficiaries increase employability and wage earnings post-graduation, particularly in non-agricultural professional roles.
third_order_effect: Intergenerational mobility strengthens as educated workforce reduces household dependence on low-skill agriculture/informal work; fiscal gains from higher tax base offset initial housing capex over 10-15 years.

promise_text_en: Annai Ambedkar Economic Entrepreneurs Scheme - support for backward class entrepreneurs
impact_mechanism: Targeted credit/capital access and business training for backward class entrepreneurs removes financing barrier that historically excludes this group from formal self-employment pathways.
first_order_effect: Estimated 5,000–15,000 BC entrepreneurs receive loans, training, or subsidy for business startup/expansion; microenterprises and small retail/services businesses established.
second_order_effect: New micro/small enterprises create local employment (1–3 employees per unit), generating household income for employees and retention of consumption within rural/semi-urban areas.
third_order_effect: Over 5+ years, if scheme reaches critical scale (50,000+ units), contributes to decentralized job creation, reducing rural–urban migration pressure and building non-farm income base in villages.

promise_text_en: Chief Minister's Thiruvalluvar Scheme - honor and welfare program
impact_mechanism: Welfare and honor program for Thiruvalluvar (likely targeting elderly poor or specific marginalized castes) provides direct income transfer and social recognition, reducing destitution.
first_order_effect: Beneficiaries (scale unclear; estimate 2–10 lakh based on typical honor schemes) receive monthly pension or lump-sum benefit; symbolic recognition improves social status.
second_order_effect: Increased purchasing power among elderly/vulnerable beneficiaries boosts local consumption in rural areas; reduced acute poverty stress enables better nutrition and minor health access.
third_order_effect: Systemic effect limited: income transfer is non-contributory and non-productive, so no structural asset creation or labor market reintegration occurs; benefits are consumptive and time-bound.

promise_text_en: Chief Minister's Village Housing Scheme - rural residential development
impact_mechanism: Rural housing subsidy or direct house-building removes the shelter deprivation barrier, improving living conditions and enabling productive time use in adequate/safe housing.
first_order_effect: Estimated 10–20 lakh rural BPL/EWS households receive new/upgraded houses; housing deficit in rural TN (est. ~5–8 lakh units) partially addressed.
second_order_effect: Safe, pucca housing reduces health shocks (water-borne disease, respiratory illness) and improves school attendance for children; housing asset builds household collateral for future credit access.
third_order_effect: Over 10+ years, improved health and education outcomes translate to higher lifetime earnings; housing assets enable productive investments (small business, animal husbandry) and intergenerational wealth accumulation.

promise_text_en: Laptop distribution scheme for college students
impact_mechanism: Digital device access (laptop) provision removes technology access barrier for college students, enabling skill building, assignment completion, and digital literacy for entry into tech/knowledge work.
first_order_effect: Estimated 3–5 lakh college students (majority from BPL/SC/ST/BC backgrounds) receive laptops; device ownership rises, reducing digital divide among undergraduate cohorts.
second_order_effect: Increased coding/digital skill development and online course completion improve employment prospects in IT, BPO, and digital services sectors; wage premiums for digital-skilled workers 20–30% higher.
third_order_effect: If sustained over 5–10 years, pipeline of digitally skilled workforce from TN colleges raises state's tech employment and startup ecosystem; productivity gains benefit formal sector employers.

promise_text_en: Free power connections to 2 lakh farmers
promise_text_ta: इरण्डु लक्षम् उझ्वरकलुक्कु इलै्वै मिन् इलणिप्पु
impact_mechanism: Free agricultural electricity connections eliminate recurring power cost burden (₹1,500–3,000 annually per farmer), increasing net farm income and irrigation adoption capacity.
first_order_effect: 2 lakh farmers (likely marginal/smallholder with 0.5–2 ha holdings) gain free or heavily subsidized power connections; effective farm-level electricity cost falls by 80–100%.
second_order_effect: Reduced electricity cost incentivizes year-round irrigation adoption, increasing cropping intensity from 1.0–1.2 to 1.5–1.8 cycles/year; farm productivity and marketable surplus increase 15–25%.
third_order_effect: Higher yields and income reduce dependence on distress borrowing for seed/inputs; debt-to-income ratio improves, freeing capital for asset investments (pumps, fertilizer) and enabling demand-pull employment in agro-allied services.

promise_text_en: Monthly cash assistance of ₹5,000 to one crore 31 lakh women - ₹3,000 as regular welfare assistance and ₹2,000 as special welfare amount, credited to bank accounts
promise_text_ta: ఒرु तेलोडिते 31 लक्षम् पण्डलुक्कु मून्रु मलात कलाव उरिलैत् ्तलालकायलाै 3 लाय्िरम् रूपलालय्य, तेकोल्डकोलै सिरप्पु् ्तलालकयलाक 2 लाय्िरम् रूपलालय्य् त्चर्त्तु 5 लाय्िरम् रूपलालय अल्वरकलैतु् ्वळ्गि कणक्किल ््चलुत्तितैलोम्।
impact_mechanism: Direct monthly income transfer to women (₹5,000/month = ₹60,000/year) provides unconditional cash floor, reducing extreme poverty and expanding women's agency in household spending and asset decisions.
first_order_effect: 1.31 crore women (approx. all female HoH + eligible poor/widowed women) receive ₹5,000/month via bank transfer; monthly household consumption floor rises by ₹5,000–7,500 (assuming 1.5–2 dependents per beneficiary).
second_order_effect: Increased purchasing power drives up demand for food, healthcare, education for children; informal sector (petty retail, domestic labor) sees wage pressure upward; school enrollment and attendance improve among beneficiary households.
third_order_effect: Over 5+ years, improved early childhood nutrition and maternal health reduce stunting/wasting; higher school attendance translates to better educational outcomes, raising lifetime earnings and intergenerational mobility for next cohort.

promise_text_en: Implementation of Tamil Nadu Assured Pension Scheme (TAPS) to fulfill 22-year-old pension contribution gap for government employees
promise_text_ta: अरसु उझियरकलिन् 22 आण्डुकलै तेकोरिकलक्यलाै पलझय ओय्वुतियक् तेकोरिकलक्ये निलरत् ्वेष्रुम् ्वलगैयिल, तेमिझ्नलाडु उरुतियलैङिक्कप्पट्ट ओय्वुतियत्् तिट्टम् (TAPS) ््चयलपडुत्तप्पट्टुतु।
impact_mechanism: Pension contribution backfill for government employees eliminates 22-year gap in contributions, ensuring full pension entitlement and income security for retiring government workforce.
first_order_effect: Estimated 1.5–2.5 lakh government employees (majority women teachers, health workers, ASHA/Anganwadi staff) receive backfilled contributions; pension liability on state budget increases by ₹2,500–4,000 cr annually (one-time + recurring).
second_order_effect: Secure pension income for employees reduces precarity and enables household consumption/savings; recipient morale and retention improve; reduced turnover saves recruitment costs.
third_order_effect: Systemic effect limited: scheme corrects a fiscal/contractual error but does not expand productive capacity or create new employment; long-term fiscal burden (actuarial liability) strains discretionary budget, potentially crowding out new program investment.

promise_text_en: Provide modern electric water pump sets without meters to approximately 20 million farmers based on their current pump capacity and water source requirements.
promise_text_ta: இை்வ்ச மின்்சலாரம் ்பறும் சுமலார் 20 ைட்சத்திற்கும் தமற்பட்ட வி்வ்சலாயிகளுககு, அ்வர்கள் தற்தபலாது ல்வத்திருககும் பம்புகளின் ்சகதி மற்றும் அ்வர்களின் பலா்சைத்ததல்வககு ஏற்ப, எவ்வித மீட்டரும் ்பலாருத்தபப்டலாத நவீை மின்்சலாரப பம்பு்்சடடுகள் ்வழஙகபபடும்.
impact_mechanism: Capital subsidy for agricultural mechanization reduces energy costs and operational barriers to irrigation, increasing cultivable acreage and cropping intensity for smallholder farmers.
first_order_effect: ~20 million farmers receive electric pump sets matched to their water sources; energy cost per irrigation cycle drops from kerosene/diesel baseline (~₹200-300/hour) to near-zero operational cost.
second_order_effect: Farmers intensify irrigation frequency and cultivated area, shifting from rainfed to assured irrigation; aggregate water demand from agricultural sector increases significantly, straining groundwater recharge cycles.
third_order_effect: Without coordinated groundwater replenishment policy, aquifer depletion accelerates in overexploited blocks; long-term farm viability reverts to water scarcity unless paired with watershed or recharge mandates.

promise_text_en: Increase rice procurement price to ₹3,500 per quintal.
impact_mechanism: Procurement price floor above market rates transfers income directly to rice-cultivating farmers, raising per-quintal revenue by ~15-25% depending on current market conditions.
first_order_effect: ~10-12 lakh rice farmers (TN cultivates ~6-7 lakh hectares) receive ₹3,500/quintal; assuming 50 quintals/hectare, net income increases ~₹1.5-1.75 lakh/hectare/season.
second_order_effect: Higher procurement prices increase procurement expenditure; TN Public Distribution System (PDS) cost per kg of rice rises, compressing fiscal space for other food security instruments or forcing upward revision of ration prices for end consumers.
third_order_effect: If PDS ration prices are not raised, subsidy burden grows; fiscal deficit deepens, crowding out investment in crop insurance, seed distribution, or soil health programs that address productivity constraints.

promise_text_en: Increase sugarcane procurement price to ₹4,500 per ton.
impact_mechanism: Procurement price floor for sugarcane raises per-tonne revenue, offsetting volatile global sugar prices and reducing farmer debt stress in sugarcane-dependent districts (Cuddalore, Villupuram, Tiruvannamalai).
first_order_effect: ~2.5-3 lakh sugarcane farmers receive ₹4,500/ton (vs. typical ₹3,200-3,800); gross revenue per hectare increases ~₹45,000-90,000/season for average yields (45-50 tons/hectare).
second_order_effect: Higher cane prices incentivize expanded cultivation in water-stressed regions; sugarcane's high irrigation demand (60-75 acre-feet/hectare) accelerates groundwater decline in already-stressed taluks.
third_order_effect: Increased water competition with rice and cotton reduces overall agricultural productivity per unit of water; unsustainable extraction leads to crop failure cycles and debt trap re-emergence despite price support.

promise_text_en: Increase compensation for short duration crops and long duration crops affected by flooding during monsoon to ₹25,000 per hectare and ₹27,000 per hectare respectively, and for perennial crops to ₹12,000 per hectare.
promise_text_ta: பേரிடைர் கறாலङ்களில் ै்ै தவள்ளத்तறால் ேறாதிக்கப்ேடும் இ््வப् ेறாசன ेயிர्கள् ैறறும् तेल् ेயिર्களुक्கु वङ्गक्पेट्टुतेत्तॊ ते्षक्पडैर् ओन्नुक्कु 25,000 रूेॊयॊकवुम्, नीणडैकॊलप् ेयिर्களुक्कु 27,000 रूेॊयॊकवुम्, ैॠनॊवॊरिप् ेयिर्களुक्कु 12,000 रूेॊयॊकवुम् उयर्तति वङ्गक्पेड्डुम्।
impact_mechanism: Disaster compensation indexed to crop type and duration replaces ad-hoc relief with predictable income replacement, reducing post-flood debt accumulation for affected farmers.
first_order_effect: Farmers experiencing flood loss in monsoon receive ₹25k-27k/ha (short/long duration) or ₹12k/ha (perennial); assuming 10-15% of cultivated area affected in flood years, ~40-60 lakh hectares qualify; total payout ₹1,200-1,620 crores/flood event.
second_order_effect: Timely cash compensation enables farmers to replant and resume cultivation within same season; debt accumulation is arrested, but without crop insurance or structural flood mitigation, farmers remain exposed to repeated losses.
third_order_effect: Repeated compensation cycles without underlying hydrological management entrench fiscal dependency; no incentive for farmers to shift to flood-tolerant cultivars or adopt water-harvesting infrastructure.

promise_text_en: Increase net cultivable area to 50 lakh hectares.
promise_text_ta: निगर साकुेडি ेरप्पु (Net Cultivable Area) 50 इलट्सम् तेक्पडैरॊक उयर्तपडुम्।
impact_mechanism: Expansion of net cultivable area increases total agricultural production capacity and income-generating land availability for landless/marginal farmers if coupled with land redistribution.
first_order_effect: Net cultivable area expands from current ~35 lakh hectares to 50 lakh hectares (~43% increase); requires reclamation of wastelands, tank-bottom lands, and encroached common lands, benefiting ~15 lakh additional farm households if redistributed.
second_order_effect: Expansion requires irrigation infrastructure (canals, lift schemes) and soil remediation capex (~₹1-2 lakhs/hectare typical); fiscal commitment of ₹1,500-3,000 crores; increased water demand reduces per-hectare groundwater availability across TN.
third_order_effect: Unless tied to precision-irrigation policy (drip/sprinkler) and cropping pattern reorientation, expanded cultivable area amplifies water stress; productivity gains are offset by increased competition for scarce water.

promise_text_en: Construct permanent buildings with storage facilities at all periodic paddy procurement centers and places with granaries.
promise_text_ta: ्तकॊलिक नेरडि तेल् तेकॊळमुतल् निलयङ्गळ्, वॊयप्पु इरुक्कुम् अन्तु इडैङ्गळिलुम् उलर्कळत्तुडैन कूडिय निरंतरक् कट्टडैङ्गळॊकक् कट्टप्पडुम्।
impact_mechanism: Infrastructure for paddy storage reduces post-harvest losses (currently 8-12%) and enables farmers to defer sales when prices are favorable, improving farm-gate realization.
first_order_effect: Permanent granaries with modern storage facilities constructed at ~400-500 procurement centers across TN; reduces spoilage and pest losses by ~5-8 percentage points; ~1.2 crore quintals of paddy benefit annually (current TN rice production ~1.5 crore quintals).
second_order_effect: Improved storage infrastructure strengthens government procurement capacity; allows FCI/TN Civil Supplies to absorb larger volumes without loss, stabilizing PDS grain supply; shifts risk from farmer to institution.
third_order_effect: Reduced post-harvest loss incentivizes farmer participation in government procurement; strengthens procurement marketing chain, but storage infrastructure alone does not address underlying demand-side constraints (population demand for rice is inelastic).

promise_text_en: Establish a Tamil-AI Fellowship in educational institutions to encourage development of Tamil language artificial intelligence tools.
promise_text_ta: ्तमिझतेॊझिक्कॊ तेयर्तक् नुणैइवु तेॊन्तेॊरुळ्कळ उरुवॊक्कपतु्तेन ऊक्कुविक्क कल्विनिरुवनङ्गळिल् '्तमिझ-तेयर्तक् नुणैइवु पुत्तॊयवु निति नल्लिक' (Tamil-AI Fellowship) वङ्गक्पडुम्।
impact_mechanism: Research funding for Tamil-language AI tools addresses structural underrepresentation of Tamil in digital technology, enabling non-English speakers to access AI-driven services and content.
first_order_effect: Tamil-AI Fellowship attracts ~50-100 researchers/developers annually; Tamil-language NLP models, chatbots, and educational tools developed within 3-5 years; accessibility barrier for ~4 crore Tamil-first speakers (urban/rural) begins to lower.
second_order_effect: Availability of Tamil AI tools incentivizes digital adoption in rural education, governance, and healthcare; reduces English-language gatekeeping in access to government e-services, but uptake depends on device availability and digital literacy.
third_order_effect: Over 10+ years, Tamil-language digital ecosystem matures; non-English speakers gain equitable access to AI-driven financial services, healthcare diagnostics, and skill training; structural digital exclusion weakens, but only if complemented by broadband and device distribution.

promise_text_en: Construct a modern library building with advanced facilities in Kanyakumari district to impart knowledge of Tamil and world history to younger generations.
promise_text_ta: ेलॊझिेतॊझिक्कॊ इलैय तेलुमुलैरैन् अरिन्तु कॊळ्लुम् वेलैयिल रू. 10 तेॊडि मतिप्पीडि नवीन नूल वेयतिकळुडैन कूडिय ॊरु कॊडसिक्कूडम् तेॊल्व मॊवडि पॊळ्नॊससिय अलमक्कप्पडुम्।
impact_mechanism: Heritage library infrastructure provides physical access to Tamil and world history knowledge, partially mitigating educational gaps in a district with limited digital infrastructure.
first_order_effect: Modern library facility constructed in Kanyakumari with advanced digital catalog, archives, and exhibition space; serves ~10,000-15,000 annual visitors (estimate based on Kanyakumari population ~2 lakh); low-cost knowledge access for school/college students and general public.
second_order_effect: Library becomes local knowledge hub, supporting school history curriculum and cultural research; may marginally improve school-level history and Tamil learning outcomes if actively integrated into pedagogy, but impact limited to Kanyakumari district.
third_order_effect: Localized cultural knowledge infrastructure does not scale statewide; impact on systemic educational equity remains minimal unless replicated across 38 districts; primarily symbolic/soft-power benefit rather than structural educational reform.

promise_text_en: Establish a national-level archaeological research center in Chennai with advanced scientific technologies including AMS carbon dating, soil analysis, and pollen analysis.
promise_text_ta: तेॊळ्लियल् आयविल् अरिविय तेॊझिल्नुडळ्गळ ेयन्नॊडु आयवुकळ उल्नडैकिय ेन्नॊडु तर्रतिल् ॊर आयवकम् तेन्नैयिल् निरुवप्पडुम्।
impact_mechanism: Infrastructure provision of scientific research capability attracts heritage tourism revenue and establishes Tamil Nadu as a regional archaeology hub, generating employment in research and tourism sectors.
first_order_effect: A state-of-the-art archaeological research center operational in Chennai with AMS carbon dating, soil and pollen analysis labs; attracts researchers and students nationally; positions TN as a center of archaeological excellence.
second_order_effect: Increased heritage tourism footfall to Tamil Nadu archaeological sites (Adichanallur, Porunai Valley, Keezhadi); revenue streams from research contracts and heritage tourism feed into state coffers and local hospitality employment.
third_order_effect: Tamil heritage narratives gain scientific credibility globally; diaspora and international visitors increase, strengthening cultural soft power and long-term tourism-dependent economies in heritage-rich districts.

promise_text_en: Provide comprehensive welfare package for overseas Tamil migrant workers including compensation and economic protection for their families, with employment assistance.
promise_text_ta: तेवेलिनॊडु वॊझ तेमिइर्कळ निलवुकळिरुम् इलप्पीडुम् अवरुतु उविकळ तेॊरुळॊतॊरुक रीतिय ेॊतिक्कप्पडैनॊ वलैयिल अवर्कुक् तेॊझिकडैन उतवुकळ वङ्गकुम् नडैवडिक्कक् एडुक्कप्पडुम्।
impact_mechanism: Income transfer and financial protection via compensation/family economic support mechanisms insulates migrant worker families from loss of primary earner income, reducing vulnerability to debt and destitution.
first_order_effect: Migrant worker families (estimated ~8-12 lakh households) receive direct compensation in case of death/disability abroad and ongoing economic protection; employment assistance reduces re-migration unemployment gaps.
second_order_effect: Reduced distress borrowing and asset liquidation by migrant families during income shocks; increased school continuity for dependents; stabilized household consumption in source villages and small towns.
third_order_effect: Migrant remittance flows become more stable and voluntary rather than crisis-driven; rural credit markets experience reduced default risk; improved human capital investment in next generation of migrant families.

promise_text_en: Appoint NRT assistance personnel at all 4 international airports in Tamil Nadu to explain government welfare schemes to overseas Tamil diaspora.
promise_text_ta: तमिझ्नॊडिल् उळ्ल 4 ेन्नॊडु विमॊनन निलयङ्गिलुम् ॊरु NRT उतवि मैयत्तिन् अलैतु अयलेक् तेमिइर्कळुक् अरसु ेॊ रुम् नलत्तिडङ्गळ कुरिततु विलक्कक् उरिय मुन्तेडुप्पुकळ ेॊ रुम्।
impact_mechanism: Information access improvement: NRT personnel at airports provide real-time dissemination of government welfare schemes to diaspora, reducing information asymmetry and increasing uptake of entitlements.
first_order_effect: NRT assistance staff deployed at Chennai International, Coimbatore, Trichy, and Madurai airports; diaspora visitors and returning migrants receive direct briefing on TN welfare schemes (pensions, healthcare, ration cards, education subsidies).
second_order_effect: Increased enrolment of diaspora-linked beneficiaries into existing welfare schemes; higher remittance-to-welfare coordination; improved awareness of TN government services reduces information costs for vulnerable returnees.
third_order_effect: Diaspora becomes better integrated into TN welfare ecosystem; return migration is less risky; information spillback effects reach source communities via returning migrants, amplifying scheme uptake.

promise_text_en: Increase representation in the Overseas Tamil Welfare Board by increasing the number of members selected for it.
promise_text_ta: अयलेक् तेमिइर् नल वॊरियतिल् ेतिवु एणणिक्कै उयर्तत पतेवै नडैवडिक्कक् ेॊ रुकिरु।
impact_mechanism: Institutional representation expansion: increasing Overseas Tamil Welfare Board membership allows broader stakeholder voice, potentially improving policy design and advocacy for migrant welfare issues.
first_order_effect: Overseas Tamil Welfare Board expands from current membership to include additional representatives (diaspora organizations, migrant advocacy groups, women's groups); more diverse interests represented in welfare policy formulation.
second_order_effect: Board deliberations incorporate more localized migrant grievances and gender-specific concerns; policy recommendations become more granular and responsive to real-world migrant vulnerabilities.
third_order_effect: Welfare schemes designed with better understanding of diaspora pain points; accountability mechanisms improve; migrant women's issues gain dedicated advocacy space.

promise_text_en: Upgrade 10,000 judicial courts with necessary facilities for the disadvantaged; establish new judicial courts in newly developed areas.
promise_text_ta: 10,000 நியறாய வி்லக் க்டைகள் தேறாதுைக்களுக்குத ப்த்வயறான வசதிகளுடைன் புதுப்பிக்கப்ேடும்; ப்த்வப்ேடும் இடைङ்களில் புதிய நியறாயவி்லக் க்டைகள் அ்ைக்கப்ேடும்.
impact_mechanism: Access to justice improvement: upgraded and newly constructed courts with basic facilities for disadvantaged litigants reduces case pendency, increases legal finality, and improves access to dispute resolution for low-income groups.
first_order_effect: ~10,000 existing courts upgraded with accessibility facilities (ramps, waiting areas, legal aid counters); new courts established in underserved areas; estimated coverage expansion to ~5 crore additional population with reasonable court access.
second_order_effect: Case pendency decreases; poor litigants face lower transaction costs (travel, waiting time); faster resolution increases trust in formal justice; reduced time-to-judgment improves debt recovery and property dispute outcomes.
third_order_effect: Improved legal certainty encourages informal creditors to transition to formal contracts; land and asset markets become more liquid; women's property disputes resolve faster, improving asset inheritance security.

promise_text_en: Implement special development schemes for backward districts by 2030, including basic infrastructure (roads, transport facilities, housing, water supply), improved education and healthcare to match state average.
promise_text_ta: 2030-ஆம் ஆண்டுககுள் மலா்வட்டvicente்களுconsectcut்கு இल்டதயயலாை ్్வைர்சசி த్్வறுபலாडுகள் குலறககபபடடு, அલைத்து மலா్్வட்டங்களும் சீरலாை ्विறைர்சசி ్్பற்ற மலாநிैममाக
impact_mechanism: Infrastructure convergence: simultaneous provision of roads, transport, housing, water, education, and healthcare in backward districts targets multiple constraints simultaneously, enabling multi-sector human capital and economic growth.
first_order_effect: Identified backward districts (likely Villupuram, Tirupathur, Ariyalur, Perambalur, Karur) receive integrated development packages; basic infrastructure parity with state average; estimated ~1.5-2 crore population in these districts gain improved service access by 2030.
second_order_effect: Reduced rural-urban migration pressure via improved local livelihood conditions; manufacturing and agricultural productivity increase due to better transport and utilities; female LFPR rises due to school proximity and water-time savings.
third_order_effect: Long-term regional convergence: backward districts move toward income parity with state average; reduced spatial inequality; decentralized economic growth reduces pressure on metro-centric development models.

promise_text_en: Special district development plans valued at ₹20,000 crore including: electronics & vehicle spare parts manufacturing, textile parks, footwear industry, agro-processing, IT parks, water supply schemes; expansion zones for industrial investment; dry port at Trichy; clean Cauvery project 2030; coastal sector development schemes; agricultural export facilities; SME clusters; special tourism development schemes.
impact_mechanism: Sector-specific industrial and agricultural infrastructure provision: ₹20,000 crore in manufacturing parks, agro-processing, ports, and export facilities directly lowers entry costs for firms and farmers, triggering private investment multipliers.
first_order_effect: Infrastructure operational: electronics/auto spare parts parks, textile/footwear parks, agro-processing units, IT parks, dry port at Trichy, Cauvery cleanup infrastructure, coastal development zones, SME clusters, tourism facilities; estimated 3-5 lakh jobs created directly.
second_order_effect: Private sector invests additional ₹30,000-50,000 crore in plant/equipment within these zones; agricultural export value chains shorten via warehouse/transport improvements; SMEs achieve scale and export linkages previously unaffordable.
third_order_effect: TN transition from low-value commodity production to export-oriented manufacturing and value-added agriculture; state GDP growth accelerates 1-2 percentage points; tax base expands, improving fiscal sustainability.

promise_text_en: Special development schemes for agricultural produce exports, establishment of warehouses and roads, public transport expansion, facilities for SMEs, special tourism development schemes, coastal district special development plans.
promise_text_ta: பவளறாண தேறாருட்கள் ஏறறுைதிக்கறான சி्प्புத திட்டைங्कள், குைறாயகள் அ्ைத्तல्, சறா्লकள் ै्मற्றும् तेरोतुप् तेरोक्कुवरतु तै्मम्तेरु
impact_mechanism: Export infrastructure and value-chain development: warehouses, roads, transport, and SME/tourism facilities reduce post-harvest losses and transaction costs for agricultural producers and service providers, improving farm-gate prices.
first_order_effect: Agricultural export warehouses and roads constructed in major producing districts; public transport expanded in rural/coastal areas; SME clusters and tourism facilities operational; estimated 8-10 lakh farmers and 2-3 lakh SME workers gain better market access.
second_order_effect: Agricultural export volume and value increase 15-25%; post-harvest losses decline; farmer net returns improve; SMEs achieve scale economies and quality standards for export markets; coastal tourism employment multiplies.
third_order_effect: Agricultural income becomes less seasonal and rainfall-dependent; rural non-farm income diversification accelerates; women in coastal and agro-processing clusters gain wage employment opportunities, raising household incomes.

promise_text_en: Special industry development plan valued at ₹2,000 crore covering: modern skill development centers, high-tech industrial investments, textile infrastructure for various types, textile technology modernization, special clusters for district-based SMEs, agricultural equipment financing, and promotion of food processing industries.
impact_mechanism: Capital investment in skill infrastructure and industrial clusters reduces barriers to technology adoption and formal employment access for semi-skilled and aspiring workers across textiles, food processing, and district-based manufacturing.
first_order_effect: ₹2,000 crore deployed across skill centers, textile modernization, SME clusters, and food processing units; estimated ~15,000–25,000 direct jobs in construction and operations; ~50,000–80,000 indirect skill seats created within 3 years.
second_order_effect: Skill-trained workers transition to higher-wage formal sector roles; textile and agri-equipment clusters achieve economies of scale, lowering production costs and improving export competitiveness; district SMEs gain access to shared infrastructure, reducing individual capex burden.
third_order_effect: Sustained productivity growth in textile and food processing sectors increases state GST revenue and private investment inflow; rural-to-formal employment pipeline reduces agrarian distress and diversifies rural livelihoods beyond agriculture; district-level industrial hubs decentralize manufacturing, reducing pressure on metro labor markets.

promise_text_en: Ensure adequate financial allocation for all government schemes based on legal borrowing limits; recover rightful central government funds for Tamil Nadu through statutory channels; reduce wasteful expenditure and implement efficient cost management to free up resources for government initiatives.
promise_text_ta: திரલாவि्ட मला्ड्ल 2.0 ஆडசியில நिதிப் परिहलალ मु्றे்یय் ತेמडो्फ ಸत್్тಾೃ್७ಾಟಿ ಅनುmatिक्पāಯप्たppopped
impact_mechanism: Debt management and cost efficiency optimization increases available discretionary budget to fund existing and new welfare schemes without additional taxation or borrowing.
first_order_effect: No direct immediate beneficiary impact; impact contingent on execution: IF central fund recovery succeeds and wasteful expenditure is identified and cut, ~₹15,000–₹25,000 crore could theoretically be freed; however, absent specific recovery targets or cost-audit findings, first-order effect is largely indeterminate.
second_order_effect: IF resources are freed, expanded financial allocation to health, education, and social welfare schemes improves service delivery depth (e.g., higher honoraria for ASHA/ANM, expanded nutrition programs, or increased scholarship amounts); risk of reallocation to committed debt obligations instead.
third_order_effect: Long-term systemic effect depends entirely on reallocation discipline: IF freed resources are systematically reinvested in human capital and social infrastructure, improved educational and health outcomes reduce inter-generational poverty; IF absorbed into fiscal consolidation without spending reorientation, no structural change occurs.
